title: crocanthemum aldersonii
intro: Crocanthemum aldersonii, also known as Arizona rosemary or desert rosemary, is a flowering plant in the aster family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its fragrant leaves and stems, which have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Crocanthemum aldersonii include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Crocanthemum aldersonii include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a carrier oil.
